title: sebastiania brasiliensis var. divaricata
intro: Sebastiania brasiliensis var. divaricata, also known as the Divaricata Sebastiania or 'Pau-de-Pique', is a plant species native to Brazil, classified in the family Euphorbiaceae. The medicinal properties of Sebastiania brasiliensis var. divaricata lie in its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which make it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Sebastiania brasiliensis var. divaricata is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used topically in creams and ointments.
